steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts resin (effective but slow, due to long curing time) with plastic sheets, which match resin's stabilizing utility while allowing near-instantaneous embedding. The question asks what conclusion is best supported by this speed-with-quality advantage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘an embedded hair's cross-sectional shape without reducing the researchers' likelihood of obtaining perpendicular cuts.’: frames plastic as merely preserving the status quo rather than introducing a speed improvement. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the efficiency of preparatory steps before sectioning a hair without compromising the hair's cross-sectional shape.’: directly captures the speed advantage (efficiency) paired with maintained quality (no compromise to shape). ✓
  • Choice C — ‘the rate at which hairs can be embedded before sectioning because no stabilizing agent is used.’: contradicts the passage, which confirms plastic does act as a stabilizer. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the researchers' likelihood of obtaining perpendicular cuts while simplifying the process of preparing a hair for sectioning.’: like A, treats the outcome as preserving rather than improving efficiency. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
